notes: Extremely low price, MicroPython firmware has stopped active updates (last version 1.22.2). Only recommended for simple WiFi projects with extremely limited budgets

onboard_peripherals.name: LED
onboard_peripherals.notes: Onboard indicator light (D4/GPIO2), lights up at low level. Can be freed in most projects

notes: I2C/SPI can be mapped via software (all GPIOs can be used), but only 1 hardware channel
GPIO 0/2/15 are strapping pins, their level at startup affects the operating mode
GPIO 6-11 are occupied by internal Flash, cannot be used externally
GPIO 16 is a special wake-up pin, only usable during Deep Sleep
GPIO 1/3 are UART0 (serial debug), avoid repurposing them
Analog input is only on GPIO0 (A0), range 0-1V (approximately 0-3.3V after internal 100k/220k voltage divider)
All GPIOs can only output 12mA at high level (ESP32 is 40mA), a resistor is needed to drive LEDs
WiFi and GPIO share internal resources, try not to perform GPIO operations simultaneously under heavy WiFi load
